description_long: This is a large, historic-style steam-powered warship with a red and black displacement hull and a beige upper deck. The ship features two tall masts, one forward and one aft, each equipped with spars and rigging, as well as a prominent central smokestack funnel. The deck is outfitted with several lifeboats, visible gun turrets, and other naval fixtures, suggesting a military or armored cruiser design. The bow is sharply pointed, while the stern is rounded, and the hull sits low in the water, indicating a vessel built for stability and endurance. The superstructure is minimal, with a single central bridge and a few raised platforms. The ship's exterior is clean and well-maintained, with no visible registration markings or flags.
description: A large, historic steam-powered warship with a red and black hull, two masts, a central smokestack, and multiple lifeboats and gun turrets on a beige deck.
description_view_0: Side profile showing the full length of the ship, two masts, central funnel, lifeboats, and deck fixtures.
description_view_1: Front (bow) view highlighting the pointed bow, symmetrical deck layout, and forward mast.
description_view_2: Opposite side profile, mirroring view 0 but with slight differences in visible deck equipment and lifeboat arrangement.
description_view_3: Rear (stern) view showing the rounded stern, aft mast, and arrangement of lifeboats and deck structures.
attribute_hull_design: The hull is a displacement type with a sharply pointed bow and a rounded stern, painted red below the waterline and black above. The hull sits low in the water, with a smooth, continuous sheer line and no visible bulbous bow or keels. The sides are relatively straight, and the overall shape is optimized for stability and endurance.
attribute_superstructure_layout: The superstructure is minimal, with a single central bridge and a few raised platforms. There are two tall masts, one forward and one aft, and a central smokestack funnel. Lifeboats and gun turrets are symmetrically arranged along the deck, with open walkways and minimal enclosed spaces.
